Given below are two statements : One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Butan–1–ol has higher boiling point than ethoxyethane.

Reason R : Extensive hydrogen bonding leads to stronger association of molecules.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements : One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Butan–1–ol has higher boiling point than ethoxyethane.

Reason R : Extensive hydrogen bonding leads to stronger association of molecules.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: A is true but R is false
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is false but R is true
solution:
Butan-1-ol can undergo hydrogen bonding due to the presence of an -OH group, ethoxyethane (also known as diethyl ether) can also participate in hydrogen bonding.

Even though there are no hydrogen atoms directly bonded to a highly electronegative atom (like F, O, or N) in ethoxyethane, the molecule still has polar C-O bonds that can lead to dipole-dipole interactions with other polar molecules.

Owing to intermolecular hydrogen bonding in butanol, it has higher boiling point than ethoxyethane.

question:

A) Polarising power of cation

B) Extent of distortion of anion

C) Polarisability of the anion

D) Polarising power of anion
solution: The percentage of covalent character in an ionic bond is determined by the polarization power of the cation, the polarizing ability of the anion, and the degree of distortion of the anion's electron cloud that occurs due to the electric field of the cation. The polarization power of a cation refers to its ability to distort the electron cloud of the anion in the ionic bond. So the options A,B,C are correct.

solution:

chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
q: The correct order of electronegativity for given elements is
question: The correct order of electronegativity for given elements is
solution:
On the periodic table, electronegativity generally increases as you move from left to right across a period and decreases as you move down a group. As a result, the most electronegative elements are found on the top right of the periodic table, while the least electronegative elements are found on the bottom left.

Element             Electronegativity

P                         2.1

C                           2.5

Br                          3.0                   

At                           2.2

Hence, the correct order is Br > C > At > P.

q: The correct order of spin only magnetic moments for the following complex ions is
question: The correct order of spin only magnetic moments for the following complex ions is
solution:
MnBr42- is a tetrahedral complex. The electronic configuration Mn2+ise2t23 and it has 5 unpaired electrons.

∴μ=5(5+2)=35 B. M.

CoF63- is an octahedral complex and it is high spin complex. The electronic configuration of Co3+ is  t2g4eg2, it has 4 unpaired electrons

∴μ=4(4+2)=24 B.M. 

Mn(CN)63- is an octahedral complex and it is low spin complex. The electronic configuration Mn3+ is t2g4eg0 it has two unpaired electrons

∴μ=2(2+2)=8 B.M 

[Fe(CN)6]3- is an octahedral complex and it is low spin complex. The electronic configuration of Fe3+ is t2g5eg0 and it has one unpaired electron.

∴μ=1(1+2)=23 B.M 

Hence the correct answer is option 3

question:
The titration curve of weak acid vs. strong base with phenolphthalein as indicator is shown below. The Kphenolphthalein =4×10-10

Given: log 2 = 0.3

The number of following statement/s which is/are correct about phenolphthalein is _______

A. It can be used as an indicator for the titration of weak acid with weak base.

B. It begins to change colour at pH = 8.4

C. It is a weak organic base

D. It is colourless in acidic medium
solution:
Phenolphthalein is an organic acid and can be represented as HPh.

HPh (colourless) ⇌H++Ph- (Pink) 

Using Henderson equation for phenolphthalein

pH=pKIn+logPh-[HPh]

At equivalence point  Ph-=[HPh]

∴pH2=pKIn=-log[4]+10=-0.6+10=9.4

Hence at (9.4±1)PH , phenolphthalein starts changing colour. Phenolphthalein is colourless in acidic medium and pink in basic medium. Phenolphthalein indicator distinguish the pH change between 8 to 10. Therefore, it is used for strong acid and strong base titration or weak acid and strong base titration. Hence, A and C are incorrect statements.

The number of following statement/s which is/are incorrect is ______

A) Line emission spectra are used to study the electronic structure

B) The emission spectra of atoms in the gas phase show a continuous spread of wavelength from red to violet.

C) An absorption spectrum is like the photographic negative of an emission spectrum

question:
The number of following statement/s which is/are incorrect is ______

A) Line emission spectra are used to study the electronic structure

B) The emission spectra of atoms in the gas phase show a continuous spread of wavelength from red to violet.

C) An absorption spectrum is like the photographic negative of an emission spectrum

D) The element helium was discovered in the sun by spectroscopic method
solution:
The emission spectrum of a element is the spectrum of frequencies of electromagnetic radiation emitted due to an atom or molecule making a transition from a high energy state to a lower energy state. The energy of the emitted photon is equal to the energy difference between the two states. There are many possible electron transitions for each atom, and each transition has a specific energy difference. This collection of different transitions, leading to different radiated wavelengths, make up an emission spectrum. Each element's emission spectrum is unique.

The emission spectra of atoms in the gaseous phase do not show a continuous spread of wavelength from red to violet, rather they emit light only at specific wavelengths with dark spaces between them.

An absorption spectrum is like the photographic negative of an emission spectrum. The element helium was discovered in the sun by spectroscopic method.

question: If the solution curve of the differential equation (y-2logex)dx+(xlogex2)dy=0, x>1 passes through the points e, 43 and (e4, α), then α is equal to _______
solution:
Given,

(y-2logex)dx+(xlogex2)dy=0, x>1

⇒2xlnxdydx+y=2lnx where logex=lnx

⇒dydx+y2xlnx=1x

Which is linear differential equation,

So, I.F=e∫12xlnx=lnx

Now, solution of the equation is given by,

y·lnx=∫lnxxdx

⇒y·lnx=23lnx32+C    ...(i)

Given, eq.(i) passes through point e, 43

So, C=23

Hence, solution of differential equation will be,

ylnx=23lnx32+23

Also given, above equation passes through point (e4, α)

αlne4=23lne432+23

⇒2α=23432+23

⇒α=13×8+13

⇒α=3

question: The number of arrangements of the letters of the word "INDEPENDENCE" in which all the vowels always occur together is
solution:
There are 5 vowels in the given word which are 4E's & 1I.

Since they have to always occur together we take them as a single object E E E E I for the time being.

This single object together with 7 remaining object will account for 8 objects.

There 8 objects in which there are 3N's & 2D's can be arrangement in 8!3!2! ways.

Corresponding to each of their arrangements the 5 vowels E,E,E,E & I which can be arranged in 5!4!

Hence, required number of arrangements.

=8!3!2!×5!4!=16800

Hence this is the correct option.

q: The number of ways, in which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> girls and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> boys can be seated at a round  table so that no two girls sit together is
question: The number of ways, in which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> girls and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> boys can be seated at a round  table so that no two girls sit together is
solution:
Given,

7 boys and 5 girls are to be seated around a circular such that no two girls to be seated together,

Now we know that n objects can be arranged in a circle in (n-1)! ways.

Let us first arrange 7 boys in circular arrangement in 7-1! ways.

Now there will be 7 gaps.

So let us select any 5 gaps out of 7 gaps and arrange 5 girls in the chosen gaps. This can be done in C57×5! ways.

Hence, required arrangements are 6!×C57×5!

=6×5!×7×62×5!

=1265!2.

question: Proton <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and electron <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mo>)</mo></math> will have same de-Broglie wavelength when the ratio of their momentum is (assume, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>m</mi><mi>p</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>1849</mn></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>m</mi><mi>e</mi></msub></math>)
solution:
The De Broglie wavelength is λ=hmv

The momentum is given by p=mv

The momentum of proton is pp=mpvp

The momentum of electron is  p'=meve

Since h is the Planck's constant and it is given that both wavelengths are same, i.e.

λp=λe, so pp=p'.

The work done in rotating dipole by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>180</mn><mo>°</mo><mo> </mo></math>in this field will be_______<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mJ</mi></math>.
question: An electric dipole of dipole moment is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mo>×</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> placed in a uniform electric field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mo>×</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>3</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> in such a way that dipole moment is along electric field. The work done in rotating dipole by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>180</mn><mo>°</mo><mo> </mo></math>in this field will be_______<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>mJ</mi></math>.
solution:
The work done for rotating a dipole is given by the change in the potential energy. The potential energy is given by

U=-p→·E→

W =∆U = Uf−Ui

So, the work done is 

 W=-pEcos180°-(-pEcos0°)=2pE=2×6×10-6×1.5×103=18 mJ

q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: If E be the total energy of a satellite moving around the earth, then its potential energy will be  E2.

Statement II: The kinetic energy of a satellite revolving in an orbit is equal to the half the magnitude of total energy E.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: If E be the total energy of a satellite moving around the earth, then its potential energy will be  E2.

Statement II: The kinetic energy of a satellite revolving in an orbit is equal to the half the magnitude of total energy E.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
solution:
Let m be the mass of the satellite.

The potential energy of the satellite is 

U=-GMmr

The velocity of the satellite is given by 

v=GMr

Kinetic energy is given by

K=mv22=m2GMr=GMm2r

The total mechanical energy is the sum of potential and kinetic energy. So,

T=K+U=GMm2r-GMmr=-GMm2r

Hence, both the statements are false.

q: The weight of a body on the earth is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Then weight of the body when taken to a depth half of the radius of the earth will be:
question: The weight of a body on the earth is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Then weight of the body when taken to a depth half of the radius of the earth will be:
solution:
The acceleration due to gravity changes with depth from the surface, which is given by

g'=g1-dR   ...(i)

The given data is 

W=400 Nd=R2

Multiplying equation (i) with the mass we get the weight.

mg'=mg1-R2R=mg12⇒W'=400 N2=200 N

If the force acting on the particle at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo> </mo><mo>+</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo> </mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> . Then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> will be:
solution:
The equation for velocity is 

v→=2t i^ + 3t2 j^

Thus, the acceleration is 

a→=dv→dt=d(2t i^+3t2 j^)dt=2 i^+6t j^

At t=1 s, a→=2 i^ + 6 j^

The force is written as F→=ma→=2 i^+6 j^2=i^ +3 j^

Comparing it with the given value of force, x=3.

The path of the charge particle will be
question: A charge particle moving in magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>,</mo></math> has the components of velocity along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> as well as perpendicular to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>. The path of the charge particle will be
options: helical path with the axis perpendicular to the direction of magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>
helical path with the axis along magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>
straight along the direction of magnetic field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>
solution:
The formula to calculate the radius of the path followed by the charged particle under an external magnetic field can be written as

r=mvBq   ...1

The perpendicular component of velocity results in circular motion, while the parallel component results in linear motion. Hence, the path of the charged particle is helical in nature, the axis of which lies along the direction of the magnetic field.

q: The magnetic intensity at the centre of a long current carrying solenoid is found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>3</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the number of turns is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> per <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi></math>, then the current flowing through the solenoid is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
question: The magnetic intensity at the centre of a long current carrying solenoid is found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>3</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the number of turns is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn></math> per <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi></math>, then the current flowing through the solenoid is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
solution:
The magnetic intensity H is defined as the product of the number of turns per unit length in a coil n and the current that it carries I.

The magnetic field of a solenoid is given by B=μ0ni

H=Bμ0=μ0niμ0⇒H=ni   ...(i) 

The given data is 

H=1.6×103 A m-1

n=8 per cm

Substituting the values in equation (i)

⇒1.6×1000=81100×i

⇒i=2 A

q: Certain galvanometers have a fixed core made of non magnetic metallic material. The function of this metallic material is
question: Certain galvanometers have a fixed core made of non magnetic metallic material. The function of this metallic material is
options: to oscillate the coil in magnetic field for longer period of time
to bring the coil to rest quickly
to produce large deflecting torque on the coil
to make the magnetic field radial
solution: Certain galvanometers have a fixed core made of nonmagnetic metallic material. When the coil oscillates, the eddy currents generated in the core oppose the motion and bring the coil to rest quickly. Eddy currents are loops of electrical current induced within conductors by a changing magnetic field in the conductor according to Faraday’s law of induction. Eddy currents flow in closed loops within conductors, in planes perpendicular to the magnetic field.

question: A cylindrical wire of mass<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mo>±</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>01</mn><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> has length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mo>±</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>04</mn><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mo>±</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>03</mn><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. The maximum error in its density will be
solution:
The formula to calculate the density of the material of the wire is given by

ρ=MπR2L   ...1

Hence, the formula to calculate the percentage error in calculating the density of the material can be written as

dρρ×100=dMM+2dRR+dLL×100   ...2

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (2) to calculate the required percentage error.

=0.010.4+2×0.036+0.048×100=4%

solution:
Using parallelogram law of vector addition, the formula can be written as

Anet=A12+A22+2A1A2cosθ

The data given is 

A1→=A, A2→=A2

Since the vectors are perpendicular cos90°=0

⇒Fnet=A2+A24=5A2
